LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE'S DBA BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIAS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT NOTICE OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. "BECAUSE THERE IS BUT ONE COMPLAINT IN A CIVIL ACTION [CITATION], THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT MOOTS A MOTION DIRECTED TO A PRIOR COMPLAINT'THUS, ONCE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IS FILED, IT IS ERROR TO GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON A CAUSE OF ACTION CONTAINED IN A PREVIOUS COMPLAINT." (STATE COMPENSATION INS. FUND V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4TH 1124, 1131.) ON OCTOBER 23, 2018, THE COURT GAVE PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT BY OCTOBER 31, 2018. DEFENDANT MAY RENEW THE MOTION AGAINST THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IF APPROPRIATE. ALTERNATIVELY, IF THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE INSTANT MOTION BEING HEARD IN DECEMBER AND THE CASE IS NOT DESIGNATED COMPLEX, DEPARTMENT 302 WILL HEAR THE MOTION IN DECEMBER. IF THE PARTIES WISH TO HAVE THE MOTION HEARD IN DECEMBER, THEN THEY SHALL MEET AND CONFER AND IMMEDIATELY INFORM THE COURT OF THE DATE THEY DESIRE A HEARING. IF THE COURT DOES NOT RECEIVE A DECEMBER DATE, THEN THE MOTION WILL GO OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/SRB)

JENSEN HAS LEAVE TO FILE HIS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 31, 2018. IT WILL NOT BE DEEMED FILED. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO GRANT THIS MOTION BASED ON CALIFORNIA'S POLICY OF LIBERALLY ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AT ANY STAGE OF A CASE AND THE ELIMINATION OF ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE BY THE CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE. IN AN EFFORT TO AVOID ANY FURTHER TRIAL CONTINUANCES, THE COURT IS SETTING DEADLINES FOR THE FILING AND HEARING OF A CLASS CERTIFICATION MOTION. IN ADDITION, IF THE CASE IS DESIGNATED AS COMPLEX THE DATES ARE SUBJECT TO CHANGE PER THE COMPLEX JUDGE. COUNSEL FOR MS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant California Physicians Service Dba Blue Shield Of Californias Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE's Dba Blue Shield Of Californias MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication.

Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is OFF CALENDAR as moot. "Because there is but one complaint in a civil action [citation], the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint'Thus, once an amended complaint is filed, it is error to grant summary adjudication on a cause of action contained in a previous complaint." (State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) On October 23, 2018, the court gave plaintiff leave to file an amended complaint by October 31, 2018. Defendant may renew the motion against the amended complaint if appropriate. Alternatively, if the parties stipulate to the instant motion being heard in December and the case is not designated complex, Department 302 will hear the motion in December. If the parties wish to have the motion heard in December, then they shall meet and confer and immediately inform the court of the date they desire a hearing. If the court does not receive a December date, then the motion will go off calendar.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Weltin, Esq. 510-856-4421 for Plaintiff Lance Jensen through Court Call.
Genevieve Casey, Esq. 510-269-7998 of Feinberg Jackson Worthman & Wasow for Plaintiff Lance Jensen.
Christopher Rheinheimer, Esq. 415-291-7468 of Manatt Phelps & Phillips LLP for Defendant California Physicians dba Blue Shield of California.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues trial continuance creates prejudice to Blue Shield. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LANCE JENSEN'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Lance Jensen's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted, the trial date is continued to December 9, 2019, all discovery deadlines are triggered off the continued trial date, and Mr. Jensen is required to file a motion for class certification no later than March 4, 2019 to be heard no later than April 15, 2019. Mr. Jensen has leave to file his first amended complaint no later than October 31, 2018. It will not be deemed filed. The court exercises its discretion to grant this motion based on California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage of a case and the elimination of any legal prejudice to defendant California Physicians' Service by the continuance of the trial date. In an effort to avoid any further trial continuances, the court is setting deadlines for the filing and hearing of a class certification motion.

In addition, if the case is designated as complex the dates are subject to change per the Complex Judge.

Counsel for Ms. Jensen to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to the sixth and eighth causes of action in plaintiff Lance Jensen's complaint is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. Mr. Jensen's letter to the Labor Workforce Development Agency, attached as Exhibit 1 to the complaint, satisfies the notice requirement for recovery of civil penalties under California's Private Attorneys General Act as to all Labor Code claims alleged in the complaint. Liberally construing the complaint, the eighth cause of action is adequately pleaded. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.
